// Localization reference.

// --- Resource files ---
// Properties/Strings.resx          → base (e.g. English)
//   Greeting = "Hello"
// Properties/Strings.vi.resx       → Vietnamese
//   Greeting = "Xin chào"
// Properties/Strings.fr.resx       → French
//   Greeting = "Bonjour"

// Type-safe access (auto-generated designer wrapper):
label1.Text = Properties.Strings.Greeting;

// --- Set the culture BEFORE building localized forms (Program.Main) ---
using System.Globalization;
using System.Threading;

var culture = new CultureInfo("vi");
Thread.CurrentThread.CurrentUICulture = culture;   // which translation loads
Thread.CurrentThread.CurrentCulture   = culture;   // number/date/currency format
Application.Run(new MainForm());

// --- Localizing a form via the designer ---
// 1. Form.Localizable = true
// 2. Form.Language = "vi"  → designer writes Form1.vi.resx
// 3. Translate control Text for that culture
// Runtime loads satellite assemblies:  vi\<App>.resources.dll

// To switch language at runtime, set the culture then re-create the form.
